name: Biscotti Cioccolato
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons instant espresso or coffee powder
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F.
2. Toast almonds in a shallow pan for 12 minutes. Cool.
3. Reduce heat to 300°F. Adjust racks to divide the oven into thirds. Line two cookie sheets with foil, shiny side up.
4. Sift flour, cocoa, baking soda, salt, and espresso powder together.
5. Finely shred chocolate using a food processor with a metal blade.
6. Add 1/2 cup of sifted ingredients and process for 20-30 seconds until the chocolate is fine.
7. In a large bowl, beat eggs and extracts just to mix. On low speed, add the chocolate mixture, remaining sifted ingredients, and sugar. Beat to mix. The dough will be very stiff.
8. Stir in almonds.
9. Turn dough onto a floured surface. Form a mound. Cut into half. Flour each half and form into sausage shapes that are 12 inches long.
10. Place sausage shapes on baking sheets diagonally.
11. Bake two sheets at a time for 50 minutes, reversing the sheets top to bottom and front to back once to ensure even baking.
12. Remove sheets from oven. Let cool for 10 minutes.
13. Transfer baked strips to a cutting board. With a serrated knife, cut strips on a sharp angle into cookies that are 1 inch wide.
14. Carefully place cookies, cut side up, on cookie sheets and bake again at 300°F for 40 minutes, turning them upside down after 15 or 20 minutes until the cookies are completely dry and crisp.
15. Turn off heat, open the oven door, and let them cool in the oven. Store in airtight canisters.
